title: CryptoCbGuard<'a> lifetime tie prevents use-after-free of raw pointer in global wolfCrypt table
description: wc_CryptoCb_RegisterDevice stores a raw *mut void pointer (to whClientContext) in wolfCrypt's global CryptoCb dispatch table. CryptoCbGuard<'a> ties its lifetime to the Client via PhantomData<&'a mut Client>, which makes the borrow checker enforce that the Client cannot be dropped or moved while the guard is alive. Without the 'a lifetime parameter, a caller could drop the Client while the guard is still live, leaving a dangling pointer in the global table; any subsequent wolfCrypt operation tagged with DEV_ID would invoke the callback with an invalid context, causing UB. A future contributor might propose removing the lifetime parameter to simplify the type, or making CryptoCbGuard own a Client. The borrow-based design is correct: it has zero overhead, expresses the invariant at the type level with no runtime cost, and avoids transferring Client ownership.
